evidence: 4038h copies one of the saved print/style banks into VV21/VV22/VV1F while classifying printable host bytes. Those banks are A2/CA/CB, CC/CD/CE, and 2D/2E/2F depending on the character range and table state.
notes: The low three bits later become the active VV3A selector. The direct producers of the saved bank bytes are still indirect or table-driven.

evidence: Reset and service/print setup paths copy VV1F into VV31; 2065h and 2C66h later load VV31 and store it to VV3A.
notes: Appears to preserve the primary active selector across line/layout setup.

evidence: 111Bh copies VV31 into VV32 when EE44h is nonzero; 2A32h and 2C24h copy the current VV3A into VV32 before later movement/layout work. 2997h and 29A4h restore VV32 into VV3A.
notes: Appears to preserve an alternate active selector while the render/motion path temporarily changes VV3A.

evidence: VV3A is loaded from VV1F during reset/service setup, from VV31 at 2067h/2C68h, and from VV32 at 2997h/29A4h. Render code masks VV3A & 07h at 21EDh/22D7h/23F1h; the scheduler copies the same selector byte into VV6F through the 5679h state-copy path.
notes: Selector values and the paired carriage/render tables are decoded in data/lq500_3c_vv3a_mode_selector.tsv.

evidence: 5679h copies source+2 into VV6F; 563Ch ORs VV6F with 04h before normal scheduler entry; 5715h indexes 72B3h by VV6F & 07h; 564Dh uses VV6F.1 to halve EF64 into EF79.
notes: Immediate 563Ch normal-scheduler records are constrained to indices 4..7, but queued/restored state can carry other low-bit values.

evidence: Startup/init write VV15=0Fh or 07h to F003h. CALT 00B8 vectors to the 51EDh AND helper; CALT 00BA vectors to the 51E9h OR helper; both update VV15 and write F003h through 51F2h. 540Dh maps selected state-byte bit 7 to F003 bit 0, and 5625h maps VV61.0 to F003 bit 1.
notes: Manual Table 2-7 speed/excitation names still require correlating bit polarity and VV3A/VV6F selector records; F003 call paths are decoded in data/lq500_3c_f003_control_paths.tsv.
